title: mesomelaena stygia var. preissii
intro: Mesomelaena stygia var. preissii, also known as the Western Australian plum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myoporaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and berries, which have been used traditionally to treat various ailments, including fever and rheumatism, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Mesomelaena stygia var. preissii include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and berries in water or oil, and can be used as a tea, tincture, or topical application.
